questions.question: How many terms are there in an A.P. whose first and fifth terms are -14 and 2, respectively, and the sum of terms is 210?
questions.explanation: a = -14, a+4d = 2 => 4d = 16 => d = 4 The sum of an AP is (n/2) {2a + (n - 1)d} Substituting we get, 210 = (n/2) {2 X -14 + (n - 1)4} => 210 = (n/2) (-28 + 4n - 4) => 4n² - 32n = 420 => n² - 8n - 105 = 0 => (n+7)(n-15) = 0 => n = 15, -7 Since number of terms cannot be negative, n=15.

questions.question: 50 liters of milk-solution is mixed with 60 liters of 70% milk solution to produce a new solution having milk concentration 15 percentage points more than that in the fifty-liter solution. Find the milk concentration in the final solution.
questions.explanation: Let the milk concentration in Fifty liters of milk solution be x% Then ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1389/image003.jpg) => 15/ (55-x) = 6/5 => 75 = 330-6x => 6x = 255 => x = 42.5% => Hence, milk concentration in the final solution = 42.5 + 15 = 57.5% => Ans is (a)

questions.question: What is the value of (a³ + b³ + c³) if (a + b + c) = 10, (a² + b² + c²) = 38 and abc = 30?
questions.explanation: Since, (a³ + b³ + c³) - 3abc = (a + b + c)(a² + b² + c² - ab - bc - ca) (a + b + c)² = 10² (a² + b² + c² + 2ab + 2bc + 2ca) = 100 38 + 2(ab + bc + ca) = 100 2(ab + bc + ca) = 100 - 38 = 62 (ab + bc + ca) = 31 (a³ + b³ + c³) - 3abc = (a + b + c)(a² + b² + c² - ab - bc - ca) (a³ + b³ + c³) - 3 * 30 = √100 * (38 - 31) (a³ + b³ + c³) = 70 + 90 = 160

questions.question: If p = (5 - 2√3)/(2 - √3), then what is the value of 2(p + 1)/(p - 3√3)?

A ladder 15 m long reaches a window which is 9 m above the ground on one side of a street. Keeping its foot at the same point, the ladder is turned to other side of the street to reach a window 12 m high. Find the width of the street.
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/ibpsimg/1260/image018.png) Let AC be the width of the road. AB² = 15² - 9² = 144 AB = 12 BC² = 15² - 12² = 81 BC = 9 So, width of the road = 12 + 9 = 21

questions.question: The cost of levelling and turfing a square field at Rs. 160 per hectare is Rs. 2624.40. The cost of surrounding it with a railing costing 25 paise per metre is: (Note: 1 hectare = 10000 sq.m)
questions.explanation: Cost of levelling and turfing = Cost per hectare X Area of the field 2624.40 = 160 X Area Area of the square filed = 16.4025 hectares 1 hectare = 10000 sq.m Hence, area = 164025 sq.m Let the side of the square filed be S S² = 164025 S = 405 m Cost of railing = 4 X 405 X 0.25 Cost of railing = Rs 405 Option 4

questions.question: The coordinates of the point P which divides externally the line joining (1, -3) and (-3, 9) in the ratio 1: 3.
questions.explanation: Let the required point be P(x, y) Then, x = (1 * -3 - 3 * 1) / (1 - 3) = 3 y = (1 * 9 - 3 * -3) / (1 - 3) = -9 The required point is (3, -9)

questions.question: A straight line joining (2,4) and (4,-4) is perpendicular to line joining (16,4) and (8,z). Find the value of z?
questions.explanation: Two lines are said to be perpendicular if the product of their slopes are equal to -1. M₁ = (-4 - 4)/(4 - 2) = -4 and M₂= (z - 4)/(8 - 16) Now M₁*M₂ = -1 So, -4*(z - 4)/(-8) = -1 z - 4 = -2 Therefore z = 2.

questions.question: P has 40 coins of 10 paise denominations. While Q has 20 coins of 50 paise denominations. R has 30 coins of 25 paise denominations while S has 35 coins of 20 paise denominations. What is the average paise per person?
questions.explanation: P has 40 × 10 = 400 paise Q = 20 × 50 = 1000 paise R = 30 × 25 = 750 paise S = 35 × 20 = 700 paise Average would be 712.5 paise only. Hence, option e.

questions.question: In a test consisting of 500 questions, Neelam answered 70% of the first 200 questions correctly. What percent of the other 300 questions, does she need to answer correctly to get a total of 80% correct questions in the entire test?
questions.explanation: Neelam needs to answer a total of 80% of 500 = 400 questions correctly, of which she has answered 140 questions correctly out of 200 already. From the rest of the 300 questions, she needs to answer 400 - 140 = 260 questions correctly. So, required percentage = 260/300 × 100 = 86.67%.

questions.question: In ∆xyz, xy = 10,yz = 10,and Angle a = 84 ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/ibpsimg/1260/image019.jpg) What is the degree measure of Angle Z?
questions.explanation: Since XY=YZ=10, then ∆XYZ is an isosceles triangle and ∠X=∠Z. ∠Y=84°, since it forms a vertical angle with the given angle. Hence, the measure of ∠Z=48°. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/ibpsimg/1260/image021.jpg) ∠X + ∠Y + ∠Z = 180° or, ∠X + 84° +∠Z = 180° 2(∠Z) +84° =180° or, 2(∠Z) = 96° or, ∠Z= 48° Therefore, the measure of ∠Z=48°.

questions.question: Income of X through services is what percentage of income of Y through Exports?
questions.explanation: GDP of X = 69120 x 5/9 = 38400 GDP of Y = 69120 x 4/9 = 30720 From the graph, we get the following data: XYTotal Agriculture122888601.620889.6 Services69124915.211827.2 Manufacturing84487372.815820.8 Exports61445529.611673.6 Miscellaneous46084300.88908.8 Total384003072069120 Required percentage = 6912/5529.6 x 100 = 125%

questions.question: What is the ratio of GDP accounted for X from Agriculture and Exports to the GDP accounted for Y from Manufacturing and Services?
questions.explanation: GDP of X = 69120 x 5/9 = 38400 GDP of Y = 69120 x 4/9 = 30720 From the graph, we get the following data: XYTotal Agriculture122888601.620889.6 Services69124915.211827.2 Manufacturing84487372.815820.8 Exports61445529.611673.6 Miscellaneous46084300.88908.8 Total384003072069120 Required ratio = (12288+6144)/(4915.2+7372.8) = 18432/12288 = 3:2

questions.question: GDP accounted for Y from Agriculture is what percentage greater than the GDP accounted for X from Manufacturing?
questions.explanation: GDP of X = 69120 x 5/9 = 38400 GDP of Y = 69120 x 4/9 = 30720 From the graph, we get the following data: XYTotal Agriculture122888601.620889.6 Services69124915.211827.2 Manufacturing84487372.815820.8 Exports61445529.611673.6 Miscellaneous46084300.88908.8 Total384003072069120 Required percentage = (8601.6-8448)/8448 x 100 = 153.6/8448 x 100 = 1.82%

questions.question: From which sector is the GDP accounted for both the countries together the third highest?
questions.explanation: GDP of X = 69120 x 5/9 = 38400 GDP of Y = 69120 x 4/9 = 30720 From the graph, we get the following data: XYTotal Agriculture122888601.620889.6 Services69124915.211827.2 Manufacturing84487372.815820.8 Exports61445529.611673.6 Miscellaneous46084300.88908.8 Total384003072069120 As can be seen from the table, the GDP accounted from services is the third highest.

questions.question: Ramu runs a shop where he sells articles each of which are of equal marked prices. Ramu had bought each of these articles at equal cost price. Even after selling the articles at a discount of 20%, Ramu earns a profit of 20%. If on a particular day Ramu earned a profit equal to cost price of 100 articles by selling articles at marked price, how many articles did Ramu sell on that day?
questions.explanation: From the given information, Marked Price * (1 - 20/100) = Cost Price * (1 + 20/100) 0.8 * marked price = 1.2 * cost price Marked Price = 1.5 * cost price Profit on selling each article = (1.5 - 1) * cost price of each article = 0.5 C.P Let number of articles sold be n. Now, Profit = C.P of 100 articles → (0.5 C.P)n = 100 C.P → n = 100/0.5 = 200 Hence, Option 3 is correct.

questions.question: A well of 11.2 meter diameter is dug 8 meter deep. The earth taken out is spread all round to it to a width of 7 meter to form a circular embankment. Find the height of this embankment?
questions.explanation: Volume of the earth dug out = π X r² X h=22/7 X 5.6² X 8=788.48 m³ Area of embankment= π (5.6+7)²-π(5.6)²=π(12.6²-5.6²)=22/7 (158.76-31.36) =22/7 (127.4)=400.4 m² Height of embankment =(Volume of earth)/(Area of embankment)=788.48/400.4=1.97 m

questions.question: In a 4 km race on a course of 250 m round, A overtakes B in the middle of his 5ᵗʰ round. By what distance will A win at the same rate of running? Given that they start simultaneously from same point and in the same direction.
questions.explanation: A overtakes B in the middle of his 5ᵗʰ round. By the time A completes 4 rounds, B should complete at least 3 rounds. Otherwise, A would have overtaken B before A even completes 4 rounds. So, when A runs 4(½) rounds, B runs only 3(½) rounds (A's speed/ B's speed) = (4(½) / 3 (½)) = 9/7 When A runs 4 km, B runs 4 * (7/9) = 28/9 km A wins by (4 - 28/9) = 8/9 km

questions.question: Three men X,Y and Z can complete a work in 6 hours if they work together. After working together for 2 hours, Z leaves the group and hence rest of the work will be completed by X and Y in 8 hours. Find the time taken by Z if would alone complete the work?
questions.explanation: Portion of the work completed in an hour if X,Y and Z work together= X+Y+Z=⅙ Portion of the work completed in 2 hours if X,Y and Z work together=⅙ X 2=⅓ Portion of the work completed in rest of the time if X and Y work together=(1-⅓)=⅔ i.e.(8 X 3)/2=12 hrs Mr. X and Y can complete the task in 12 hours. Therefore (X+Y +Z)can complete the task in 6 hours. Z=(X+Y+Z)-(X+Y) →Z=12 hours

questions.question: A shopkeeper bought a shirt for Rs 560 and marked it 70% higher than its price. He offered 25% discount on the marked price. A regular customer asked for a further reduction of Rs 60 and purchased the shirt. Find the percent profit the shopkeeper made by selling the shirt.
questions.explanation: MP = 1.7 x 560 = Rs 952 Price after 25% discount = 952 x 0.75 = Rs 714 Selling price = 714 - 60 = Rs 654 Profit % = (654-560)/560 x 100 = 94/560 x 100 = 16.78%

questions.question: If cosθ + cos²θ + cos³θ = 1, then what is the value of sin⁴θ - 4sin²θ + 8?
questions.explanation: cosθ + cos²θ + cos³θ = 1 cosθ + cos³θ = 1 - cos²θ cosθ(1+cos²θ) = sin²θ Squaring both sides, we get cos²θ(1+cos²θ)² = sin⁴θ (1-sin²θ)(1+(1-sin²θ))² = sin⁴θ (1-sin²θ)(2-sin²θ)² = sin⁴θ (1-sin²θ)(4+sin⁴θ-4sin²θ) = sin⁴θ 4 + sin⁴θ - 4sin²θ - 4sin²θ - sin⁶θ + 4sin⁴θ = sin⁴θ -8sin²θ - sin⁶θ + 4sin⁴θ + 4 = 0 8sin²θ + sin⁶θ - 4sin⁴θ = 4 sin²θ(8+sin⁴θ-4sin²θ) = 4 8 + sin⁴θ - 4sin²θ = 4/sin²θ = 4cosec²θ Hence, sin⁴θ - 4sin²θ + 8 = 4cosec²θ

questions.question: Shanthanu borrowed ₹30000 from two moneylenders. He pays interest at the rate of 12% per annum for one loan and 14% per annum for the other. The total interest he pays for the entire year is ₹3720. How much did he borrow at the rate of 12% if both the amounts were borrowed at simple interest?
If tan α=¾, find the value of (3sinα - 2cosα)/(3cosα + 2sinα)
questions.explanation: The given expression is (3sinα - 2cosα)/(3cosα + 2sinα) Dividing the numerator & denominator by cosα, we get (3tanα - 2)/(3 + 2tanα) = (9/4 - 2)/(3 + 3/2) = (¼)/(9/2) = 1/18

questions.question: A and B are two alloys of brass and aluminum prepared by mixing metals in the ratio of 5:2 and 5:9 respectively. If equal quantity of alloys are melted to form a third alloy C, find the ratio of brass to aluminum in C?
questions.explanation: Let 1 kg of each one of A and B is taken to form 2 kg of C. Brass in 1 kg of A = 5/7 Aluminum in 1 kg of A = 2/7 Brass in 1 kg of B = 5/14 Aluminum in 1 kg of B = 9/14 Brass in 2 kg of C = 5/7 + 5/14 = 15/14 Aluminum in 2 kg of C = 2/7 + 9/14 = 13/14 Ratio of brass to aluminum in C = 15:13

questions.question: Find the smallest number of 5 digit which is exactly divisible by 113?
questions.explanation: Smallest number of 5 digit = 10000 On dividing, 10000 by 113, we get 56 as remainder number to be added = 113 - 56= 57 Hence, required number = 10057
